articles.title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 3 LIVE: Lok Sabha to take up Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026
articles.sum: The first two days of the session were marked by protests and sloganeering with no business conducted in both Houses so far

articles.title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Union Ministers meet Wangchuk, convey Govt.'s call to initiate Parliament debate on education reforms
articles.sum: 20 additional CRPF companies rushed in from West Bengal as people continue to gather in large numbers at Jantar Mantar

articles.title: Pinarayi Vijayan condemns arrest of Rahul Gandhi in Delhi, says Centre ‘suppressing’ protest using force
articles.sum: In a Facebook post, Pinarayi says that according to news reports, ‘a gang of thugs with lathis’ were attacking protesters along with the police

articles.title: Hospitals must not function as an extension of law enforcement: Safdarjung Hospital resident doctors
articles.sum: Residents association seeks President’s intervention in Wangchuk case; defending patients’ rights, doctors say hospitals must not be seen as places of detention or used to facilitate non-medical objective

articles.title: Difference between ‘chest’ and ‘breast’ immaterial in POCSO offences: Kerala HC
articles.sum: The accused had contended that the mere touch or catch on the chest of a child would not stand as an offence under the POCSO Act. It was argued that only touching the breast of a child with sexual intent would constitute the offence.

articles.title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul Gandhi over PM residence march
articles.sum: The BJP launched a sharp political counteroffensive against Rahul Gandhi's protest. Senior BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad criticized Gandhi's actions as irresponsible and condemnable. The BJP announced nationwide protests outside Congress offices in response to the demonstration....

articles.title: Crowds return to 'cockroach' protest after India police crackdown
articles.sum: Azadeh Moshiri reports from Delhi's Jantar Mantar, where the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has been protesting for education reforms.

articles.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
articles.sum: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

articles.title: Are England finally improving in ODI cricket?
articles.sum: After beating India 2-1, BBC Sport asks if England are finally building momentum one year out from the World Cup.

articles.title: Diamonds and 'cab to orbit': Rocket aiming to transform India's space ambitions takes off
articles.sum: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

articles.title: The hunger-striking engineer demanding education reforms in India
articles.sum: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has a long history of challenging the status quo and refusing food to highlight his causes.

articles.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
articles.sum: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.

articles.title: Live: Congress MPs arrive at Parliament in black clothes, Priyanka Gandhi leads protest
articles.sum: Live Congress MPs arrive at Parliament in black clothes Priyanka Gandhi leads protest

articles.title: Monsoon clouds swallow 95% of India, Isro satellite images show: What to expect
articles.sum: Monsoon clouds swallow of India Isro satellite images show What to expect

articles.title: Why Sharad Pawar's grand-nephew may break ranks with NCP (SCP)
